title: Homestead Foundry model registry
description: Schema for a single Azure AI Foundry (or Foundry-adjacent) model registry entry. A registry file is a JSON array of entries matching this schema. See pmo/MODEL-REGISTRY-DESIGN.md for the full design rationale and the consumption contract a consuming repo follows to resolve an id to a usable endpoint.

id.description: Stable, kebab-case identifier a consuming repo references. Never reused across two different models once published.

kind.description: Add new values here as new modalities are adopted; keep this enum authoritative, do not let consumers infer kind from id or provider.

provider.description: Prefer the vendor's own name (for example Microsoft, BlackForestLabs, OpenAI, xAI). Free text beyond a common set is fine.

deploymentName.description: The actual Foundry deployment name a caller passes as the model parameter. For an entry whose status is planned or rejected, this is the name it would use (or did use) once deployed, not a placeholder.

region.description: Azure region the deployment targets, or the region a planned deployment would target.

status.description: rejected entries are kept, with notes explaining why, so the decision is not re-researched later - never delete a rejected entry.

accessGating.description: For example none, limited-access: <url>, vendor-terms-acceptance. Use "none" literally when nothing gates access.

capabilities.description: For example text-to-image, image-edit, vision-input, word-timestamps, viseme-output.

notes.description: Short and factual. Longer rationale belongs in the doc sourceRef points at, not here.

sourceRef.description: Relative path to the spike, ADR, or backlog entry that backs this entry (for example ai/research/SPIKE-01-image-model.md).
